risk_title: Conflict-related access limitations and asset security concerns in Tigray region
risk_summary: The project faces significant challenges in Tigray region due to ongoing conflict, including inability to verify asset existence and condition, loss or damage to project resources, and limited access to financial documents from woredas.
evidence_quote: The Special Financial Audit Report of the Project part implemented by the Tigray Region for the period from July 8, 2020, to October 11, 2023, has been submitted. The auditor has expressed an unqualified (clean) type of audit opinion, but management letter has indicated failure to provide financial documents of Woredas, inability to verify existence, condition, and ownership of assets, and absence of assessment of lost or damaged assets.

risk_title: Conflict-related damages and resource losses in Amhara and Afar regions
risk_summary: Project management has not submitted comprehensive reports on the extent of damages or status of project resources (assets, balances, financial records) in Amhara and Afar regions affected by conflict, creating uncertainty about project asset security.
evidence_quote: In addition, it is to be recalled that we requested for comprehensive report of extent of damages and/or status of project resources (assets, balances, financial records) in Amhara and Afar regions with the mitigation measure or a detail plan to address damages/risks, but this has not been submitted to DPs. Our request remains in that there should be an update on the matter by the government.

risk_title: Security constraints limiting procurement and implementation in conflict-affected woredas
risk_summary: Internet disruptions and security-related constraints in targeted woredas have limited access to STEP platform and procurement documents, hindering timely procurement plan approvals and implementation.
evidence_quote: These issues are primarily due to security-related constraints, which have resulted in internet disruptions in targeted woredas. These disruptions have not only limited access to the STEP platform and procurement documents but have also made it difficult to receive even Excel-based documents from those areas.

risk_title: Internal conflict impeding project implementation in Amhara region
risk_summary: Internal conflict is flagged as a major challenge that impedes movement and slows implementation of WASH activities in Amhara region.
evidence_quote: The following major challenges were flagged by the region: the region has highlighted some of challenges encountered during the operation that includes price escalation, budget shortage for some of the components, limited matching fund allocation from Woredas, lack of logistics and poor staff incentive. Key Implementation Challenges: 1) Internal conflict which impedes movement

risk_title: Governance fragility and weak coordination in newly established regions
risk_summary: Newly established regions (SW Ethiopia, South Ethiopia, Central Ethiopia) show weak governance structures with low staffing levels (26-51% in some regions), delayed recruitment, and limited functional coordination among sector bureaus.
evidence_quote: Newly established regions are still underperforming in rural water supply, with rates of 27% in SWER, 46% in SER, and 51% in CER, while Amhara stands at 55%, Afar at 59%, Gambella at 57%, and Harari at 58%. Despite identifying the slow progress in these regions during the last mission, there has been no significant progress.

risk_title: Weak institutional capacity and staffing gaps in SW Ethiopia region
risk_summary: SW Ethiopia region has only 26% of required staffing in place, with 24 vacant positions including critical roles in environmental and social safeguards, procurement, and financial management.
evidence_quote: However regional variations exist mainly in SW Ethiopia region where only 26% of the required staffs are in place and South Ethiopia region where the Procurement specialist recruitment has been delayed.

risk_title: Tensions between government and development actors over matching fund contributions
risk_summary: Significant delays and shortfalls in government matching fund contributions across multiple regions are creating bottlenecks in project implementation and causing payment delays for completed activities.
evidence_quote: Delay in Matching Fund Contribution has become a bottleneck dragging implementation of some large value contracts and causing payment delays for completed activities.

risk_title: Political instability affecting staff retention and governance in woredas
risk_summary: High staff turnover and leadership reshuffling at woreda level, combined with low and irregular staff salaries, are undermining program governance and implementation capacity.
evidence_quote: Key Implementation Challenges: Manpower turnover (Staff & Political officials at woreda level), Low & irregular staff salary & remuneration

risk_title: Displacement and access constraints in drought-prone CR-WASH woredas
risk_summary: Climate-resilient WASH projects in drought-prone woredas face implementation challenges related to population displacement and limited accessibility during rainy seasons.
evidence_quote: Site accessibility is poor during rainy season. The Aysaeita–Afambo Multi-Village Rural Water Supply Project is currently ongoing. 17.65% physical progress was achieved in the reporting period with a total physical target of 91.88%.
